task_text: Look at the sprint 14 assignments and testing log, then tell me which tasks are at risk of missing the sprint deadline. Write your assessment to sprint14_deadline_risk.txt.

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress

HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done

Project Helios — QA Testing Log: Sprint 14
QA Engineer: Kenji Watanabe
Period: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-11 (ongoing)

=== Test Suite: Authentication Flows ===
TC-401  OAuth token issuance              PASS
TC-402  Token refresh                     PASS
TC-403  Expired token rejection           PASS
TC-404  Invalid scope rejection           PASS
Suite result: 4/4 PASS

=== Test Suite: Data Pipeline — Batch Ingestion ===
TC-501  CSV ingestion (10k records)                  PASS
TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS
TC-503  XML ingestion (5k records)                   PASS
TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS
TC-505  Date normalization (MM/DD/YYYY input)        FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-014]
TC-506  Duplicate record detection                   PASS
TC-507  Large file ingestion (500k records)          FAIL  [OPEN — HEL-D-015]
Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Test Suite: Dashboard ===
TC-601  Data table rendering (10k records)           PASS
TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS
TC-603  Chart rendering — bar chart                  NOT RUN
TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

input.content:
Sprint 14 Deadline Risk Assessment
Sprint window: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18
Assessment date: 2026-07-11

Tasks at risk of missing the sprint deadline:

1. HEL-243 — Data ingestion: date format normalization
   Assignee: Amir Hassan
   Due: 2026-07-15
   Status: Blocked
   Risk level: HIGH
   Rationale: The task is already marked Blocked, and the testing log shows open high-severity defect HEL-D-014: MM/DD/YYYY date normalization fails and affects approximately 40% of Meridian source data. The blocker depends on Meridian sample data due the same day as the task deadline, leaving little or no time for fix, validation, and retesting. Amir also owns HEL-D-015, increasing delivery risk.

2. HEL-245 — Integration test suite: data pipeline
   Assignee: Kenji Watanabe
   Due: 2026-07-18
   Status: In Progress
   Risk level: HIGH
   Rationale: The data pipeline test suite is only 5/7 passing, with two open high-severity failures: HEL-D-014 for date normalization and HEL-D-015 for large file ingestion memory overflow. Even though the task deadline is the sprint end date, completion depends on fixes from Amir and successful retesting. Current failures block clean completion of the suite.

3. HEL-246 — UAT environment: server provisioning
   Assignee: Nadia Petrov
   Due: 2026-07-16
   Status: At Risk
   Risk level: HIGH
   Rationale: The task is explicitly marked At Risk and is an 8-point item due before sprint end. No testing-log evidence indicates it has been validated or recovered. If provisioning slips, it could also affect UAT readiness for other completed work.

4. HEL-247 — Performance benchmark: dashboard load
   Assignee: Derek Okafor
   Due: 2026-07-18
   Status: Not Started
   Risk level: MEDIUM-HIGH
   Rationale: The task has not started, and the related dashboard load-time benchmark test TC-604 is not run. It may also be affected by HEL-D-015, the high-severity large-file ingestion memory overflow, because benchmark scenarios involve large data volumes. With the due date at sprint close, there is limited buffer for setup, execution, fixes, and retesting.

5. HEL-242 — Dashboard: chart rendering module
   Assignee: Tomas Rivera
   Due: 2026-07-16
   Status: In Progress
   Risk level: MEDIUM
   Rationale: Dashboard chart testing is incomplete: line chart testing is still in progress and bar chart testing has not run. The task is due 2026-07-16, leaving some time, but the remaining unexecuted tests create risk if defects are found late.

6. HEL-248 — GeoSync license renewal coordination
   Assignee: Raj Patel
   Due: 2026-07-14
   Status: In Progress
   Risk level: MEDIUM
   Rationale: This is a small 2-point task but is due soon and remains in progress. There is no direct test-log evidence of failure, but the near-term due date makes it worth monitoring.

Lower-risk / not currently at risk:

- HEL-244 — Integration test suite: auth flows: Done; authentication suite is 4/4 PASS.
- HEL-241 — Dashboard: data table component: In Progress and due 2026-07-14, but related test TC-601 data table rendering passed. Monitor because the due date is near, but current evidence does not show a blocking issue.

Overall assessment:
The greatest sprint-deadline risks are concentrated around data ingestion/data pipeline work, especially HEL-243 and HEL-245, due to two open high-severity defects and a dependency on Meridian sample data. HEL-246 is also high risk because it is explicitly flagged At Risk. HEL-247 is concerning because it has not started and depends on performance testing that has not yet run.
